TACKLES RATE PROBL EN-

pages.raw_text:
state rates on freight and passen-
ger traffic.


According to an agreed plan the
Texas legislature will take similar
action.


This attempted legislation is the
result of recent conferences in Aus
tin and Galveston between the two
state corporation commissions Ne
braska, Kansas, Arkansas and Mis-
souri, were asked to participate in
these meetings, but were not repre-
scented.


Under such an arrangement Okla
homa would fix the rates to tide
water on Oklahoma wheat, corn,
cotton and such other products as
are shipped south. In turn Texas
would regulate rates on northbouni
cattle, rice, lumber, fruits and 9th
er products. In either case both
states would agree upon the rating
before it became effective.


abolish the interstate commerce
law and place the regulation of

cessive freight rates solved at

states can regulate passenger fares
